title: ferray-stats: no scipy.stats equivalents — no t-test, ks-test, mode, skew, kurtosis, zscore
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

scipy.stats provides descriptive statistics beyond what's in NumPy: mode, skew, kurtosis, zscore, describe, trim_mean, iqr, sem, gmean, hmean. It also provides statistical tests: ttest_1samp, ttest_ind, ks_2samp, chi2_contingency, pearsonr, spearmanr. ferray-stats covers the NumPy statistical functions well but has none of the scipy.stats functionality. For a complete 'stats' crate, at least the descriptive statistics (skew, kurtosis, zscore, mode) would be expected.
